info.name: Car Showroom Management System — Production
info.description:
Production API test collection for the Car Showroom Management System, targeting the DigitalOcean deployment.

📋 PREREQUISITES
• Set the `base_url` collection variable (defaults to the production DigitalOcean URL)
• Enable Postman's cookie jar (on by default in modern Postman Desktop/Web)
• Admin must already be seeded in production (admin@example.com / admin123, or update `admin_email`/`admin_password` variables)

🔑 SESSION HANDLING
This app uses express-session with a PostgreSQL-backed store. There is no bearer token — Postman's built-in cookie jar automatically stores and replays the `connect.sid` session cookie for `{{base_url}}` after any successful login/register. No manual header work needed.

⚡ AUTO-SAVED VARIABLES
Create/register requests use request-level Tests scripts to save IDs (and the employee's credentials) as collection variables — car_id, car2_id, customer_id, rental_id, purchase_id, employee_id, rental_term_id, doc_id, image_id, image2_id, feedback_id, client_user_id. No manual copy-pasting needed if you run folders top-to-bottom.

▶️ RUN ORDER
Folders are ordered in realistic dependency order: Health → Auth → Admin User Mgmt → Customers → Cars (create/publish/images) → Car Documents → Rentals → Purchases → Rental Terms → Dealer Contact → Feedback → Public Endpoints → Cleanup.

item.name: 0. Health Check

request.description: Returns {status: 'ok', timestamp: '...'}. If this fails, the production deployment is not reachable.

item.description: Verify the production server is reachable before running any other requests.

item.name: 1. Authentication

request.description: Self-register as a client. role='client', is_email_verified=false. Triggers a verification email (no-op if RESEND_API_KEY is not set in production).
Resends the verification email for the registered client.

item.name: Verify Email (requires real token from inbox)

request.description: Verifies the raw token emailed to the client. Copy the token from the verification email link and paste it into the query param, then run this request before 'Login (Client)' will succeed.
Returns 403 if email is not yet verified. Session cookie stored automatically by the Postman cookie jar.
Confirms the client session is active.
Switches the session to admin. All subsequent admin-only folders assume this session is active — run this before folders 2-10.
Always responds with the same message regardless of whether the email exists, to prevent enumeration.

item.name: Reset Password (requires real token from inbox)

request.description: Completes password reset using the raw token emailed to the user. Marks is_email_verified=true and destroys all existing sessions for that user.

item.description: Register/login flows. IMPORTANT: run 'Login (Admin)' last in this folder so the session used by all subsequent admin folders is the admin session.

item.name: 2. Admin — User Management

request.description: Admin only. Creates a new employee user (role must be 'employee' — cannot create another admin via API). Saves employee_id.

exec: if (json.id) { pm.collectionVariables.set('employee_id', json.id); console.log('Saved employee_id:', json.id); }

request.description: Also supports ?is_active=true|false.

exec: pm.test('All results are employees', () => { pm.response.json().users.forEach(u => pm.expect(u.role).to.equal('employee')); });

request.description: Partial update of full_name and/or is_active.
Promotes the client registered in folder 1 to employee. Uses client_user_id saved at registration.
Body MUST be an empty object {} (schema is .strict()). Returns 400 if target is not currently an employee, 403 if target is admin.

Run 'Login (Client)' with employee_email/employee_password beforehand (or add a dedicated employee login request) to exercise this as a non-admin session. If run right after admin actions, this documents the expected RBAC behavior — admin session will actually get 200, so switch sessions first via the request below.

exec: pm.test('Status 403 while acting as employee', () => pm.response.to.have.status(403));

request.description: Switches session to the employee created above, to exercise RBAC checks below.
Employee has full access to car management (contrast with the users endpoint above).

exec: pm.test('Status 200 - Employee can access cars', () => pm.response.to.have.status(200));

request.description: IMPORTANT: restores the admin session for all subsequent folders.
Sets is_active=false. Kept near the end of this folder since later folders no longer need this employee.

item.name: Hard Delete (example — needs a disposable user id)

request.description: Permanently deletes a user row. Body MUST be exactly { "confirm": "DELETE" }. Cannot target admin or self. Run against a disposable test user id, not employee_id, in real usage.

exec: pm.test('Status 200 or 404 depending on prior soft-delete', () => pm.expect([200,404]).to.include(pm.response.code));

request.description: Demonstrates the FORBIDDEN guard. To truly test blocking admin deletion, target the admin's own user id (GET /auth/me while logged in as admin) instead — the admin route always rejects role='admin' deletion with 403 FORBIDDEN.

item.description: Create and manage employee accounts, plus role promotion/demotion and RBAC checks. Admin only (except the deliberate RBAC probes).

item.name: 3. Customers

request.description: At least one of phone or email is required (Zod .refine). Saves customer_id.

exec: if (json.id) { pm.collectionVariables.set('customer_id', json.id); console.log('Saved customer_id:', json.id); }

request.description: Case-insensitive ILIKE match against name, phone, or email.
Partial update — only provided fields change.

item.description: Manage customer records. Admin + employee access.

item.name: 4. Cars — Admin

request.description: Creates the main rent-listing test car. VIN is optional but must be unique. Saves car_id.

exec: if (json.id) { pm.collectionVariables.set('car_id', json.id); console.log('Saved car_id:', json.id); }

request.description: Second car, used by the Purchases folder. Saves car2_id.

exec: if (json.id) { pm.collectionVariables.set('car2_id', json.id); console.log('Saved car2_id:', json.id); }

request.description: Returns all cars regardless of is_published status.

item.name: List Cars — Filter by Listing Type

exec: pm.test('All cars are rent listings', () => json.cars.forEach(c => pm.expect(c.listing_type).to.equal('rent')));

request.description: Partial update of any car fields.

formdata.description: Select an image file (JPEG, PNG, or WebP — max 5 MB)

request.description: multipart/form-data upload → Cloudinary. Only one primary image per car (enforced by a partial unique index).

exec: if (pm.response.json().id) { pm.collectionVariables.set('image_id', pm.response.json().id); console.log('Saved image_id:', pm.response.json().id); }

formdata.description: Select an image file (JPEG, PNG, or WebP — max 5 MB)

exec: if (pm.response.json().id) { pm.collectionVariables.set('image2_id', pm.response.json().id); console.log('Saved image2_id:', pm.response.json().id); }

request.description: Promotes the secondary image; the old primary is atomically demoted.
Deletes from both the DB and Cloudinary.
After this, the car appears on GET /cars (public).

item.description: Create, update, and publish the car inventory, including image management. Admin + employee access.

item.name: 5. Car Documents

request.description: Key-value spec field attached to a car. Saves doc_id.

exec: if (json.id) { pm.collectionVariables.set('doc_id', json.id); console.log('Saved doc_id:', json.id); }

item.description: Arbitrary key-value spec fields per car (e.g., registration number, insurance expiry). Admin + employee access.

item.name: 6. Rentals

request.description: car_id must be a listing_type=rent car. Checks date overlap, snapshots rent_price_per_day, calculates total (end_date - start_date + 1 days, inclusive).

exec: if (json.id) { pm.collectionVariables.set('rental_id', json.id); console.log('Saved rental_id:', json.id); }

request.description: car2_id is listing_type=sale — renting it must be rejected.
Aug 5–10 overlaps the pending Aug 1–7 rental created above (end_date is inclusive).
Also supports car_id, customer_id, start_date_from, start_date_to.
pending → confirmed. No side effect on car status.
confirmed → active. SIDE EFFECT (atomic transaction): cars.status = 'rented'.
active cannot go back to pending.
active → completed. SIDE EFFECT: cars.status = 'available' (if no other active rentals).

item.description: Full rental lifecycle: create → confirmed → active (car becomes rented) → completed (car becomes available again), plus overlap/listing-type validation.

item.name: 7. Purchases

request.description: car2_id must be listing_type=sale. Snapshots sale_price from the cars table. Saves purchase_id.

exec: if (json.id) { pm.collectionVariables.set('purchase_id', json.id); console.log('Saved purchase_id:', json.id); }

request.description: car_id is listing_type=rent — purchasing it must be rejected.
Filter with ?car_id=, ?customer_id=, ?status=.

item.name: Mark as Paid → Car Becomes Sold

request.description: CRITICAL: uses a DB transaction to atomically set purchase=paid AND car=sold.

item.description: Purchase lifecycle: create → paid (car becomes sold, atomically), plus listing-type and availability validation.

item.name: 8. Rental Terms — Admin

request.description: Hard delete — no soft-delete for this resource.

item.description: Full CRUD for rental terms shown on the public site. Admin + employee access.

item.name: 9. Dealer Contact — Admin

request.description: Single-row table — always returns the one showroom contact record (seeded on migration).
Partial update — every field is optional/nullable. status: auto derives open/closed from the day/time fields; open/closed force it.

item.description: Manage the single-row showroom contact/hours record shown on the public site. Admin + employee access.

item.name: 10. Feedback — Admin

request.description: Run after submitting feedback in folder 11 (Public Endpoints) to see it appear here unapproved.
Saves the first unapproved feedback id for the moderation requests below.

exec: if (json.length && json[0].id) { pm.collectionVariables.set('feedback_id', json[0].id); console.log('Saved feedback_id:', json[0].id); }

request.description: Once approved, this feedback becomes visible on the public GET /feedback endpoint.
Admins moderate only (approve/hide/delete) — they never edit the customer-submitted text.

item.description: Moderate homepage testimonial feedback submitted via the public endpoint. Admin + employee access.

item.name: 11. Public Endpoints

request.description: No auth required. Only returns is_published=true cars, each with an images array.
Supports brand, model, year_min/max, price_min/max, rent_price_min/max, body_type, limit, offset.
Includes images and documents arrays.
No auth required. Returns only is_active=true terms ordered by sort_order.
No auth required. Showroom contact info, hours, and open/closed status.
No auth required. Returns only is_approved=true feedback, newest first.

No auth required, but rate-limited per IP. 'name' is server-derived (session user's full_name if logged in, else 'Anonymous') — the request body cannot set it. Starts is_approved=false (hidden until an admin approves it in folder 10).

exec: pm.test('Has confirmation message', () => pm.expect(json.message).to.equal('Thank you for your feedback'));
if (json.id) { pm.collectionVariables.set('feedback_id', json.id); console.log('Saved feedback_id:', json.id); }

request.description: No auth required. Returns active employees for a public "our team" page.

item.description: No authentication required. Run after the admin folders above have created/published data, since these read what was created there.

item.name: 12. Cleanup

request.description: Confirms the session was destroyed by logout.

item.description: Run at the very end to close out the admin session.
